name: Name Your Moves
concept: Functions — teach the robot a move once, then use it anywhere by name

learn: Great coders don't repeat themselves — they NAME things
The 🧩 teach block saves a bunch of blocks as one move, like ⭐
Then the little ⭐ block runs that whole move — anywhere, as many times as you want
Teaching a move does NOTHING by itself — it only runs when you CALL it
In real JavaScript this is a function: function star() { ... } and star() — check </>!

blocks.name: teach ⭐ star move
blocks.text: put blocks inside to save them as your ⭐ move. It sits on the side — it doesn't run on its own!

blocks.name: do star move
blocks.text: runs everything you taught ⭐ — one small block, one big move

blocks.name: teach 🌙 moon move
blocks.text: a second move for later levels — some puzzles need two!

blocks.name: old friends
blocks.text: loops and ifs work INSIDE your moves, and your moves work inside loops. Combine everything!

intro.goal: Spot the repeating pattern, teach it as a move, then call it. Fewer blocks, smarter robot!

recap: You wrote your first functions — named moves you teach once and reuse everywhere
Defining is not running: ⭐ only happens when you CALL it
You put loops and ifs INSIDE a function, and functions inside loops — that's composition, how all real software is built
In real JavaScript you wrote: function star() { moveForward(); } and called it with star();

quiz.q: What does 🧩 teach ⭐ do by itself?
choices: nothing yet — it just saves the move
quiz.why: Teaching only SAVES the move. It runs when you call ⭐.

quiz.q: ⭐ is taught as [⬆️⬆️💎]. The program runs ⭐⭐. How many squares does the robot walk?
quiz.why: Each ⭐ call runs the whole move: 2 steps + grab, twice = 4 steps.

quiz.q: Why teach a move instead of copying the same blocks again and again?
choices: you build it once and reuse it anywhere
it makes the robot walk faster
quiz.why: Reuse! Fix or improve the move in ONE place and every call gets better.

quiz.q: You teach ⭐ a great move but never place a ⭐ call block. What happens?
quiz.why: A function only runs when called. Defining is just teaching.

quiz.q: In real JavaScript, teaching ⭐ looks like…
quiz.why: That's a function definition — you saw it in the </> panel!

quiz.q: 🔁 repeat 3 times [⬆️⬆️] — how many squares does the robot walk?
quiz.why: World 2 check: the loop runs both moves 3 times — 2 × 3 = 6.

quiz.q: 🤔 if 💎 on gem? do [collect] — the robot is NOT on a gem. What happens?
choices: it collects nothing and keeps going
quiz.why: World 3 check: answer NO means the if blocks are skipped.

quiz.q: The program is ⭐🌙⭐. Which move runs LAST?
quiz.why: World 1 rule still rules: blocks run in order, one at a time.

levels.name: Teach a Move
levels.hint: NEW! Put ⬆️⬆️💎 inside 🧩 teach ⭐. Then just call ⭐⭐⭐ — three gems, one move!

levels.name: The Side-Dance
levels.hint: Remember this square? Teach ⭐ one whole side (walk 3, grab, turn), then call it 3 times.

levels.name: Star in a Loop
levels.hint: Mind-bender: put a LOOP inside your ⭐ move! Teach ⭐ = repeat 3 [move, grab], then ⭐ turn ⭐ turn ⭐.

levels.name: Two Moves
levels.hint: Two moves! Teach ⭐ the scoop (walk walk grab) and 🌙 the sidestep (turn right, step, turn left). Then ⭐🌙⭐🌙⭐.

levels.name: Zig & Scoop
levels.hint: Teach ⭐ the stair-climb (step, turn left, step, turn right). Climb twice, scoop the hallway, climb twice more!

levels.name: Smart Star
levels.hint: Teach ⭐ a SMART move: step, then if on gem — grab. Call it 6 times and it handles any hallway!

levels.name: Double Scoop
levels.hint: Gem PAIRS this time. Teach ⭐ the double-scoop and 🌙 the sidestep — then alternate.

levels.name: Star Power
levels.hint: Everything at once: teach ⭐ = repeat 3 [step, if on gem grab]. One smart side, called 3 times.

levels.name: Backwards Star
levels.hint: Same trick, other direction — you start facing DOWN. Your ⭐ works no matter which way you're going!

levels.name: Boss: Function Master
levels.hint: Boss! Teach ⭐ the stair-climb and 🌙 the smart step (move, if on gem grab). Climb, sweep, climb, sweep to the flag 🏁
